findings.title: learn:execute allowed-tools include Read/Write even though the command states agents should not manually edit .workflow/learn
findings.impact: Broader tool surface increases accidental state mutation risk; harder to enforce the CLI State API boundary.
findings.recommendation: Remove Read/Write from allowed-tools (or document the exact allowed write locations) to keep the contract enforceable.

findings.title: learn:plan uses flags.noAgent while UI/argument-hint uses --no-agent (camelCase mapping depends on the command runner)
findings.impact: If flag normalization is not guaranteed, the agent path can be inverted (unexpected template fallback or vice-versa).
findings.recommendation: Normalize flags explicitly (e.g. const noAgent = flags.noAgent ?? flags['no-agent'];) and add a doc contract test for it.

leads.description: Verify how the /learn:plan command runner maps --no-agent into flags (camelCase vs hyphen)
